sections.heading: National mortality rates were lower than in 2022
sections.body: At the national level, the 2025 five-year childhood mortality rates were lower than the same measures in 2022. Under-5 mortality declined from 26 to 22 deaths per 1,000 live births, while infant mortality declined from 22 to 18. Neonatal mortality moved from 15 to 12, post-neonatal mortality from 7 to 6, and child mortality from 5 to 3.

sections.heading: Male children had higher 2025 mortality rates than female children
sections.body: In 2025, male children had higher rates than female children on most measures. Under-5 mortality was 24 deaths per 1,000 live births for males and 19 for females, infant mortality was 19 for males and 17 for females, and neonatal mortality was 13 for males and 11 for females. Post-neonatal mortality was the same for both groups at 6, while child mortality was 5 for males and 2 for females.

sections.heading: Urban and rural under-5 mortality rates were close in 2025
sections.body: Residence differences were modest in 2025. Under-5 mortality was 22 deaths per 1,000 live births in rural areas and 21 in urban areas. The components moved in different directions: rural areas had lower neonatal and infant mortality than urban areas, while urban areas had lower post-neonatal and child mortality.

takeaways: National under-5 mortality was 22 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2025, down from 26 in 2022.
Male children had higher 2025 under-5 mortality than female children, 24 compared with 19 deaths per 1,000 live births.
Urban and rural under-5 mortality rates were nearly the same in 2025, at 21 and 22 deaths per 1,000 live births.
